channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Fired probationary workers at Commerce say their insurance is being cut early
item.description: Probationary workers who were refired this month from the Commerce Department say their health insurance is being terminated earlier than they expected.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: Partisan divide widening on US support for NATO: Pew
item.description: Among party lines, Democrats' opinion about NATO being beneficial to the U.S. has largely stayed the same, but this view has dropped off by 6 points among GOP respondents.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Rubio says State Department to restrict visas of at least 250 Nicaraguan officials
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced the State Department will restrict visas for at least 250 Nicaraguan officials, pointing to human rights violations made by the Nicaraguan government, led by co-presidents Daniel Ortega and Rosario Murillo, who are husband and wife.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Of Angel Moms and Illegals
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump taps Mark Levin, Henry McMaster to ‘revamped’ Homeland Security council
item.description: President Trump announced Thursday he has picked Fox News contributor Mark Levin, South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster and other allies to serve on a "revamped" Homeland Security Advisory Council.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota baseball lovers make a pitch to honor bygone ballpark, 2 historic St. Paul teams
item.description: The St. Paul Saints and St. Paul Colored Gophers played ball in the early 1900s at a tiny ballpark near the Capitol called the Pillbox. The Colored Gophers were a vital piece of Black baseball history nationally. A local historian is lobbying now for a public commemoration of the site.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: As Afghan Christians face deportation, faith leaders urge Trump administration to reconsider
item.description: Afghan Christians in the U.S. face imminent deportation after the termination of TPS, and faith leaders have made urgent appeals to the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump moves to invoke Schedule F to make it easier to fire some federal workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has begun making one of the controversial personnel changes for government employees that was spelled out in the conservative Project 2025 blueprint for his second term.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge blocks Trump administration from passport changes affecting some transgender Americans
item.description: A federal judge has partially blocked the Trump administration from enacting a policy that bans the use of “X” marker used by many nonbinary people on passports as well as the changing of gender markers.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Strange sell-off in the dollar raises the specter of investors losing trust in the US under Trump
item.description: Among the threats tariffs pose to the U.S. economy, none may be as strange as the sell-off in the dollar.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The Abrego Garcia case pulls Democrats into the immigration debate Trump wants to have
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — For Democrats, the Kilmar Abrego Garcia case is about fundamental American ideals — due process, following court orders, preventing government overreach. For the Trump administration and Republicans, it's about foreigners and gang threats and danger in American towns and cities.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump calls FSU shooting a ‘shame,’ says he’ll always protect Second Amendment
item.description: President Trump on Thursday called a deadly shooting at Florida State University (FSU) "terrible," but he dismissed the possibility of it spurring any changes to gun laws in the country. "Look, I'm a big advocate of the Second Amendment. I have been from the beginning.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Oklahoma House passes 'Christ is King' resolution
item.description: Every Republican present on the floor voted for the resolution and every Democrat present voted against.
item.dc:creator: Hunter Elyse/ KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Class action lawsuit challenges Trump’s crackdown on student visas
item.description: A federal judge who blocked Donald Trump’s administration from dismantling the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau has ruled that the agency can’t go forward immediately with plans to fire hundreds of employees.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump admin to cut 90% of CFPB in latest layoffs: Reports
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration is cutting nearly 90 percent of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau’s (CFPB) employees in a push to significantly downsize the watchdog agency formed to shield U.S. consumers from financial fraud and abuse.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supervisor Lawson-Remer presents 'local battleplan' during State of County address
item.description: This year’s state of the county address was delivered in Balboa Park by acting Chair Terra Lawson-Remer. She shared her proposed plan for how to move the county forward in challenging fiscal times and what she considers “a moment of democratic crisis.
item.dc:creator: Kasia Gregorczyk, Fox 5

item.title: Trump signs executive action to lower drug prices
item.description: The action is intended to build upon the existing program for Medicare drug price negotiations, which was created by the Inflation Reduction Act that passed during the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Sydney Lupkin, MPR News

item.title: Biden says Trump administration has done 'breathtaking' damage to Social Security
item.description: Former President Joe Biden warned a Chicago audience assembled Tuesday at the national conference of Advocates, Counselors and Representatives for the Disabled that the Trump administration has "taken a hatchet" to Social Security.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: States push Medicaid work rules, but few programs help enrollees find jobs
item.description: Some lawmakers are pushing to require that Medicaid recipients work in order to get or keep coverage, and some states already try to help them find jobs. But the effects of those efforts are unclear.
item.dc:creator: Sam Whitehead, Phil Galewitz, and Katheryn Houghton, MPR News

item.title: Medicaid puzzle confronts Minnesota lawmakers. Federal cuts could hit health safety net program
item.description: Republicans in Washington are working on slashing the federal budget to help pay for a slate of tax breaks. State lawmakers know it could mean a health care funding hole, but they don’t know how much they’ll have to backfill.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: 281 bills fall victim to deadline at Nevada Legislature. Here are the highlights
item.description: A total of 281 bills missed a Friday deadline to win committee passage in Carson City, the first major deadline at the Nevada Legislature this session. If that seems like a lot, consider that 875 bills are still alive.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pritzker says he's undecided about testifying before Congressional panel
item.description: The US House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform requested testimony from Pritzker, other Democrats about sanctuary laws.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: Cuomo housing plan faces scrutiny
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Candidates in the race for New York City mayor are calling out former Gov. Andrew Cuomo for his housing plan. They claim it is light on details and that part of it was written using ChatGPT.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Illinois Democrats mull next moves after strong showing in April elections
item.description: Gov. JB Pritzker and top state Democrats gathered Monday at a union hall in DuPage County to celebrate the party's near clean sweep of Illinois municipal elections two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9
